top_findings.title: Agent wrote all deliverables outside the sandbox into the user's home directory
top_findings.evidence: _agent_stdout.log states "Files created in `/Users/adriancockcroft/`"; files exist at /Users/adriancockcroft/main.py, test_api.py, README.md, requirements.txt (perms 0600, mtime 11:03-11:06). File-mutation verifier also flagged a refused write to a sensitive system path.
top_findings.suggestion: The agent must write only inside its assigned workspace/cwd. Constrain the runner to a sandboxed cwd and reject absolute/home writes; clean up the leaked ~/main.py, ~/test_api.py, ~/README.md, ~/requirements.txt.

top_findings.title: Workspace archive contains no source, README, or tests — run is not reproducible from the archive
top_findings.evidence: find run_dir -type f returns only logs/metadata (TASK.md, stack.json, scores.json, .coverage, _agent_*.log); no *.py or README.md present. The stored scores.json (test_coverage=0.97) was computed against files that live outside the archive.
top_findings.suggestion: Fail/quarantine runs whose workspace has no deliverables; scores derived from out-of-workspace files are not attributable to the archive.

top_findings.title: All CRUD endpoints return HTTP 500 at runtime — get_db dependency wrongly decorated with @contextmanager
top_findings.evidence: main.py: get_db is decorated @contextmanager then used as Depends(get_db); verified via TestClient(app) without overrides: POST /books -> 500, GET /books -> 500 (AttributeError: '_GeneratorContextManager' object has no attribute 'throw'). Only /health works (it opens its own SessionLocal, not via Depends). Tests pass only because test_api.py sets app.dependency_overrides[get_db]=override_get_db, masking the defect.
top_findings.suggestion: Remove the @contextmanager decorator so get_db is a plain generator dependency (def get_db(): db=SessionLocal(); try: yield db; finally: db.close()).
